asset_family: town forge equipment direction target and source families
source_family_role: full-screen target composition for direction review only; not a fused runtime asset

recommended_canvas.background_policy: perfectly flat chroma or true transparency only

rows.purpose: all requested reusable runtime assets for this source family
rows.item_policy: one isolated component per slot with clear gutters and no composed runtime screen

prompt: Create a production source sheet for town forge equipment direction target and source families. Source family: composed town forge fake shot. Role: full-screen target composition for direction review only; not a fused runtime asset. This is a cuttable source sheet, not a gameplay screenshot, mockup, landing page, or composed UI screen. Arrange the sheet as a row_major_grid with at least 64px outer margin and 48px gutters between all visible pixels and shadows. Row 1 isolated_assets: all requested reusable runtime assets for this source family; one isolated component per slot with clear gutters and no composed runtime screen. Leave empty chroma lanes between rows and slots. Do not compose these assets into a runtime screen. Use a perfectly flat chroma background #00ff00 or true transparency; do not use gradients, shadows, glow, or texture in the background. Keep every asset isolated with generous gutters and no overlap between shadows. Keep all labels, numbers, quest text, counters, and state values blank for runtime composition. Put unique gems, badges, medallions, locks, banners, and center ornaments in separate overlay sprites, not inside stretchable bases. Use consistent perspective, lighting, material language, and line weight across the sheet.
negative_prompt: button labels, resource counters, timer values, tutorial text, debug text, game state values, unreadable fake letters, exact reference-game UI ornaments, numbers inside cost/result slots, random letters, watermarks, labels fused into reusable backgrounds, icons fused into buttons, wrong subject, weak silhouette at gameplay size, opaque chroma-key background, baked board state, one fused screenshot used as runtime UI, shape-rectangle programmer art, text or labels baked into reusable pieces, Y-up assumptions inverted in layout notes, panel-only forge UX with no scene object anchor, readable text, fake letters, watermark, fused icons inside buttons, cropped silhouettes, merged components, busy stretch centers, non-flat chroma background, purple or red-blue halo on transparent edges
constraints: no fused icons inside button backgrounds
transparent or flat chroma-key background for isolated assets
no unique decoration inside slice9 stretch zones
no copied War of Dragons, Dragon Eternity, AQW, DragonFable, AdventureQuest, Shakes and Fidget, or Wartune UI ornament shapes
Y-up must be preserved in layout notes and runtime translation
forge/equipment meaning must be visible in the scene, not only in the right panel

relevant_asset_groups.role: single composed target for the town forge upgrade state: forge/workbench in the scene, hero and blacksmith/warden focus, Mine Lantern object/result slot, ember shard cost, route strip showing Depth 2 opened, and persistent browser-RPG frame
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: direction target only; do not treat as one fused runtime UI image
relevant_asset_groups.coordinate_policy: composition notes and future runtime layout must remain Y-up; any screen-space conversion is boundary-only

relevant_asset_groups.role: painted Old Gate forge/town workbench layer, lantern crafting focus, forge glow, and route/depth marker overlays
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: no UI text, no baked labels, no copied reference landmarks
relevant_asset_groups.layer_policy: background, forge/workbench, lantern object, ember shard glow, and route-depth highlight should be separable or reproducible

relevant_asset_groups.role: Mine Lantern, ember shard, forge hammer/anvil, unlock/depth, cost paid, equipped result icons
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: isolated icons with gutters; no numbers or labels baked into icons

relevant_asset_groups.role: isolated hero, blacksmith or Gate Warden forge pose, lantern pickup/equip marker, and small forge glow FX
relevant_asset_groups.anchor_policy: pivot or anchor must be recorded before runtime use

relevant_asset_groups.role: ornate but readable browser-RPG frame pieces for right equipment/result rail, route strip, bottom log, reward/equipment slots, and primary button bases
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: runtime text/icons only inside safe areas
relevant_asset_groups.stretch_zone_policy: centers and long edges must be repeatable; unique ornaments stay in corners or separate overlays
relevant_asset_groups.decor_overlay_policy: quest gems, banners, medallions, locks, and center ornaments must be separate overlays or fixed corner pieces, never stretched through the center

final_asset_policy.notes: Code may cut, validate, pack, and compose. Code-generated/programmer art is not acceptable as final generated UI unless an explicit exception is recorded.

acceptance_checklist: Source sheet is not a full gameplay screenshot.
Background is flat #00ff00 or true transparent alpha.
No readable text, fake glyphs, labels, numbers, or watermark.
Components have clear gutters and do not share antialias/shadow pixels.
No stretchable slice9 bases are mixed into this source family.
Ornaments that should not stretch are separate overlay sprites with visible isolation.
Icons/sprites have full silhouettes with padding for alpha trim.
Source can pass source-sheet intake before crop rectangles are trusted.
Accepted output will get a generation record with provider/model/workflow/seed or no-seed reason.
